question_title: Will a specific release date for Halo: Campaign Evolved be officially announced by June 14, 2026?
summary: The Xbox Games Showcase on June 7, 2026 falls squarely within the resolution window (deadline June 13), and Halo: Campaign Evolved is virtually certain to be featured. The game is reportedly content-complete with multiple credible leaks pointing to a July 28, 2026 launch. A game ~7 weeks from launch being shown at its publisher's biggest annual event almost always receives a specific release date. The primary risk is that Microsoft announces only a month/season ("July 2026" or "Summer 2026") without committing to a specific calendar day, which would NOT satisfy the resolution criteria. However, for retail pre-order campaigns and marketing purposes, day-specific dating is standard for games this close to launch. My probability decomposition yields ~83%: P(appears at Showcase) × P(specific date given | appears) + small contributions from pre/post-Showcase channels.

factors.description: Xbox Games Showcase on June 7 falls squarely within the resolution window (before June 13) - this is THE premier venue for Xbox date reveals

Game reportedly content-complete per insiders, with multiple credible leaks (grunt.api, Korean retailer, insider Graczdari) converging on July 28 launch - a game ~7 weeks from launch almost always gets dated
Halo is Xbox's flagship franchise celebrating its 25th anniversary - maximum marketing motivation to date it prominently at their biggest annual event
Resolution window extends 6 days past Showcase to June 13, providing additional channels (Halo Waypoint blog, store page update, social media) for date announcement
Risk: Microsoft could announce 'July 2026' or 'Summer 2026' without a specific day - this would NOT satisfy resolution criteria requiring day/month/year
Risk: Historical 12-15% rate of games getting specific dates at Xbox showcases (though biased by early-stage titles; near-launch rate is much higher)
Risk: Halo Studios known for very late date confirmations per question background - could hold back specific day even while showing the game

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.83 (83% YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

1. **The Xbox Games Showcase on June 7 is the critical event.** It falls 6 days before the resolution deadline, and Halo: Campaign Evolved is virtually certain to be featured as Xbox's flagship title.

2. **The game appears nearly ready for launch.** Multiple independent leaks converge on a July 28, 2026 release date, and insiders report the game is content-complete. Games ~7 weeks from launch at their publisher's biggest annual event almost always get specific dates.

3. **The 25th anniversary amplifies motivation.** This is a marquee franchise celebrating a milestone - Microsoft has every incentive to make a splash with a concrete date reveal.

4. **Main downside risk:** Microsoft could announce "July 2026" or "Coming this Summer" without specifying a day, which would NOT satisfy the resolution criteria requiring a specific calendar date (day/month/year). This is the primary source of uncertainty, though it would be unusual for a game this close to launch.

5. **Post-Showcase buffer:** Even if the Showcase doesn't reveal a date, there are 6 additional days (June 7-13) for announcements via Halo Waypoint, store page updates, or social media.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will a specific release date for Halo: Campaign Evolved be officially announced by June 14, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43765)
[Halo: Campaign Evolved available with Game Pass in 2026 | Xbox](https://www.xbox.com/en-US/games/halo-campaign-evolved)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, at any time between October 24, 2025 (the game's original announcement) and 23:59 UTC on June 13, 2026, Halo Studios, Xbox/Microsoft, or PlayStation publicly announces a specific calendar release date (a specific day, month, and year — e.g., "July 28, 2026") for the full retail/standard launch of Halo: Campaign Evolved on any of its confirmed platforms (Xbox Series X|S, PC, or PS5), via an official channel such as the [Halo Waypoint news page](https://www.halowaypoint.com/news/introducing-halo-campaign-evolved), the [Xbox store page for the game](https://www.xbox.com/en-US/games/halo-campaign-evolved), an official Xbox/Halo social media account, or an official press release. Otherwise the question resolves NO. An announcement of only a month and year (e.g., "July 2026"), a season (e.g., "Summer 2026"), or only an early-access/beta date without a full-launch date does not qualify. Reporting by third parties, leakers, or retailer listings does not count — only an announcement from an official Microsoft/Xbox, Halo Studios/Halo Waypoint, or PlayStation channel. If the game is officially delayed and a specific 2027 (or later) date is announced before the deadline, that still resolves YES.
fine_print: If the Xbox store page or Halo Waypoint updates its "Available 2026" tag to a specific date, that counts as an official announcement. If officially published date information is ambiguous (e.g., a placeholder date appears and is then retracted within 24 hours), it does not qualify. If no primary official channel is reachable, reporting from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) attributing a date to an official Microsoft/Halo Studios/PlayStation statement may be used.

factors.description: Xbox Games Showcase on June 7 falls within resolution window - this is the premier venue for Xbox date reveals
Game reportedly content-complete, with multiple credible leaks pointing to July 28 launch - games ~7 weeks from launch almost always get dated
Halo is Xbox's flagship franchise and 25th anniversary title - maximum marketing motivation to date it at Showcase
Resolution window extends to June 13, providing 6 days after Showcase for additional announcement channels
Risk: Microsoft could announce 'July 2026' or 'Summer 2026' without a specific day - this would NOT resolve YES
Risk: Historically only 12-15% of games at Xbox showcases get specific dates (though this stat includes early-stage games)
Risk: Halo Studios known for very late date confirmations - could delay the specific date announcement

input.anchor: Base rate for major first-party Xbox games being dated at Xbox Showcase when they're within ~2 months of launch: ~75-85%. Most near-complete flagships get specific dates at summer showcases.
input.assessment:
The question hinges almost entirely on whether Microsoft will reveal a specific calendar date for Halo: Campaign Evolved at the June 7 Xbox Games Showcase, which falls squarely within the resolution window.

Strong YES evidence: The game is reportedly content-complete, multiple credible leakers point to a July 28 release, the Showcase is 4 days away, and Halo is Xbox's flagship franchise celebrating its 25th anniversary. A game 7 weeks from launch at its publisher's biggest annual event almost always gets a specific date.

Key NO risk: The distinction between "July 2026" and "July 28, 2026" matters critically. Microsoft COULD announce a month/season window rather than a specific day. However, for a game this close to launch, a specific day is standard practice - retail partners, pre-order campaigns, and marketing all require a specific date.

The 12-15% stat about showcases is misleading here - it covers all games including those years away. For near-launch flagships, the rate is much higher. My decomposition yields ~83% YES, which feels right given the strong convergence of evidence toward a YES outcome but acknowledging genuine uncertainty about whether they'll commit to a specific day vs. a month.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor is conditional base rate for near-launch flagships at major publisher showcases. The main divergence risk is the specific-day requirement - if they say 'July 2026' that's NO. But for a game 7 weeks from launch, day-specific dating is the norm.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether Microsoft gives a specific day vs. just a month/season. 2. Whether there's an unexpected delay that changes the announcement strategy. 3. Whether the Showcase features Halo at all (low uncertainty - near certain it will).
input.tool_audit: web_search: Useful for finding current state of store pages and recent news. fetch_url: Confirmed Xbox store still shows 'Available 2026'. research(): One sub-agent couldn't access tools but the other provided excellent Showcase context. get_coherence_links: No related questions found.
input.update_triggers: If the Showcase happens June 7 and Halo is shown WITHOUT a date, probability drops dramatically. If a specific date is announced, question resolves YES immediately.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was partially effective - one sub-agent had tool failures while the other succeeded. Direct web search filled the gaps well. The key analytical challenge was estimating the probability of a day-specific date vs. a vaguer window, which required judgment about industry norms rather than data retrieval.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks Performed

### Resolution Criteria
The agent fetched the Metaculus question page (post_id 43765), fully retrieved the resolution criteria, and its factors directly engage with the key requirements: a specific calendar date (day/month/year), from official channels, between Oct 24, 2025 and June 13, 2026 at 23:59 UTC. Factor 5 specifically addresses the critical distinction between "July 2026" (which would NOT qualify) and "July 28, 2026" (which would). The agent also correctly fetched the Xbox store page to confirm it still shows "Available 2026" with no specific date as of the forecast date.

### Hallucinated Evidence Check
- **Factor 1** (Xbox Games Showcase June 7): Confirmed by multiple web search results and the worldview research entry. ✓
- **Factor 2** (content-complete, July 28 leaks): Trace shows web search results from insider-gaming.com ("reportedly nearly finished"), grunt.api decoded leak pointing to July 28, wccftech.com confirming July 28, eplayworld.com (insider Graczdari saying July 30). The claim of "content-complete" is supported by the Instant Gaming article: "the game is now complete in terms of content." The "July 28" figure is supported by multiple independent sources. ✓
- **Factor 3** (flagship franchise, 25th anniversary): Wikipedia confirms "Campaign Evolved commemorates the 25th anniversary of both the Halo franchise and the Xbox platform." ✓
- **Factor 5** (month vs. day risk): Valid risk derived from resolution criteria. Not a factual claim requiring trace support. ✓
- **Factor 6** (12-15% stat): Directly confirmed by the worldview research file `historical-rate-of-xbox-showcases-giving-specific-60023720.json`, which shows "approximately 12-15% of major first-party games shown at Xbox showcases (2020-2024) received specific release dates." ✓
- **Factor 7** (Halo Studios late confirmations): Stated in the Metaculus question's own background info. ✓
- **Anchor** (75-85% for near-launch games): Not directly sourced as a statistic, but is a reasonable inference. The worldview research shows that the games that DID get specific dates at showcases (Forza Horizon 5, Starfield 2023, Forza Motorsport 2023, CoD BO6) were all within 3-5 months of launch — and essentially all near-launch flagships in the sample got dated. The 75-85% figure is arguably conservative given this data (could be closer to 90-100% based on the small sample), but calling it "not directly sourced" would be nitpicking since the direction is right.

### Double-Counting
Factors 1 and 4 are related (both about the Showcase being within the window), but Factor 4 adds the distinct point about post-Showcase days. Factors 2 and 3 both support YES but draw on different evidence (development status/leaks vs. franchise importance). No problematic double-counting.

### Wrong-Direction Factors
All factor signs are consistent with their descriptions. Positive factors support YES, negative factors flag risks against YES. ✓

### Contradictory Assessment
The narrative assessment is consistent with the factors: strong YES evidence from the Showcase timing, game completion, and leaks, tempered by genuine uncertainty about whether they'll commit to a specific day vs. a month/season. The 83% probability aligns with the decomposition math (83.8% from the code output). ✓

### Pre-Publication Event
No pre-publication event claimed. The question was published June 3, 2026 and the Showcase hasn't happened yet (June 7). ✓

### Weak Counterargument
The agent's counterargument is substantive. It identifies: (1) Microsoft's pattern of vagueness, (2) the specific risk of "Coming this July" vs. "July 28" — which directly maps to the resolution criteria's specificity requirement, (3) quality/certification risk, (4) the possibility of decoupling the date announcement from the Showcase, and (5) the narrow 6-day post-Showcase window. This is a real alternative story, not a token gesture. ✓

### Overconfident Self-Assessment
The agent reports confidence_in_estimate of 0.55, which is appropriate. The evidence is strong directionally (Showcase happening, game near-complete, leaks pointing to July 28), but the specific-day requirement creates meaningful uncertainty that the agent acknowledges. 0.55 is well-calibrated for this evidence quality. ✓

### Probability Assessment
I independently estimate the probability as follows:
- P(Halo appears at Showcase) ≈ 0.95 (confirmed by multiple sources, it's the flagship)
- P(specific date given appears) ≈ 0.80-0.85 (near-launch flagships historically always get dated, but "Summer 2026" or "July 2026" is a real risk)
- P(date via other channels if not at Showcase) ≈ small
- Total: ~0.78-0.83

The agent's 83% is at the upper end of my range but within it. The agent's code-based decomposition yields 83.8%, which is consistent. I don't find this meaningfully wrong. The key tension is between the very high base rate for near-launch flagships getting specific dates (~close to 100% historically) and the risk of Microsoft announcing only a month/season. The agent appropriately balances these.

### Anchor Divergence
The stated anchor is 75-85%. The final probability of 83% is within the anchor range — no divergence issue.

### Tool Audit
The agent claims "research(): One sub-agent couldn't access tools but the other provided excellent Showcase context." The trace confirms: the first research sub-agent (Halo-specific query) produced a response that says "all web search and fetch tools are unavailable in this session" — it returned only training knowledge. The second sub-agent (Xbox Showcase query) produced comprehensive research with sources. This matches the agent's description. The agent then compensated with its own web searches. Tool metrics show 0 errors across 14 calls, consistent with "0 errors." ✓

### Cross-Question Consistency
The related forecast on "How many game reveals and updates will TrueAchievements count from Xbox Games Showcase 2026" (post 43734) also expects Halo: Campaign Evolved to appear at the Showcase, consistent with this forecast. No contradictions found.

### Worldview Research Contradictions
The worldview research entries on Xbox Showcase 2026 and historical date-giving rates are consistent with the agent's factors. The 12-15% overall rate is correctly cited, and the agent appropriately contextualizes it (noting it includes early-stage games, while Halo is near-launch).

### Summary
No errors found. All factual claims are grounded in the trace. The probability of 83% is reasonable given the evidence. The agent properly engages with the resolution criteria's specificity requirement and identifies the key risk (month vs. day announcement).
